title: New Hampshire man arrested in 1993 homicide of Maine woman
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Daniel Jolly, 59, was arrested in Portsmouth, New Hampshire, for the 1993 murder of Maxine Bitomski in Kittery, Maine.
Jolly knew the victim through his job at a medical supply company that serviced Bitomski, according to police.
Maine State Police renewed the investigation in 2021, leading to Jolly's arrest; extradition plans to Maine are underway.

title: Red Sox demote Brayan Bello after another disastrous start
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Brayan Bello was demoted to Triple-A Worcester following a rough start against the Orioles.
Bello has struggled as a starter this season, holding a 16.88 ERA in the first inning over several games.
Despite strong bullpen performances, the Red Sox opted to send Bello down following continued starting struggles.

title: Mass. police department decries dangerous behavior on e-bikes after 3 youth charged
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Attleboro police received 143 reports of teens riding e-bikes recklessly since November 2024.
A 16-year-old was charged after using an e-bike dangerously and confronting police in Attleboro.
Two teens attempted to steal a confiscated e-bike from police storage, leading to arrests.

title: Keep a look out for the Northern Lights over New England skies Thursday into Friday
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The aurora borealis may be visible over New England from Thursday night into Friday morning, says NOAA.
Peak viewing for the northern lights is expected between 10 p.m. and 2 a.m., avoiding light pollution is advised.
Auroras result from coronal mass ejections earlier this week; a smartphone may help capture the lights.

title: Am I required to take my new vehicle to the dealer for maintenance?
author: John Paul
bullets: Vehicle owners are not required to use the dealer for routine maintenance to retain their warranty.
Correct oil specification and quality filter use are necessary for non-dealer service to maintain the vehicle's warranty.
Newer Subaru engines may need specific oil standards, such as synthetic 0W-20 meeting Subaru specifications.

title: Worcester man gets 4 to 7 years in prison for killing neighbor; family calls sentence a ‘slap in the face’
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Enrique Canales-Martinez, 27, pleaded guilty to manslaughter for killing neighbor Jason Mayhew, receiving a 4-to-7-year prison sentence.
Mayhew was killed following an argument over loud music; Canales-Martinez shot Mayhew after he allegedly charged at him.
The victim's family criticized the sentence as too lenient, while the defense argued it was a case of self-defense.

title: Holyoke church cancels service, temporarily closes after theft
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: St. Paul's Episcopal Church in Holyoke canceled services and closed temporarily following a break-in and theft.
Stolen items included raffle prizes and food for a fundraiser supporting church renovations and a new daycare center.
A pizza party fundraiser will be held June 12 to help replace stolen items; new security measures are being implemented.

title: NFL insider: ‘Good chance’ Patriots will sign Christian Gonzalez to huge contract extension
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Christian Gonzalez likely to sign contract extension with Patriots, increasing his salary beyond $2.2 million.
Gonzalez, absent from voluntary OTAs, aims to leverage for a better contract deal before season start.
Patriots coach Mike Vrabel expects Gonzalez at mandatory minicamp, emphasizing contract talks should remain private.

title: Police investigating suspected arson at Boston’s Museum of African American History
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Police investigate a suspected arson labeled as a hate crime at the Museum of African American History.
A Juneteenth-themed package was set on fire outside the museum at 46 Joy St. in Beacon Hill, Boston.
Police seek public help to identify a white male suspect captured in surveillance images near the incident site.

title: Woman, 69, killed in Swampscott fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 69-year-old woman died after a fire in her Swampscott apartment on Thursday, authorities reported.
The fire occurred at a condominium complex on Loring Avenue and is under investigation by multiple agencies.
The woman's identity is withheld pending family notification, according to District Attorney Paul Tucker's office.
